title: CMPE 286 - LAB - 1

headings.text: Part 3: Identify HTTP Requests and Responses
Part 7: TCP 3-Way Handshake and Packet Timing

section_anchors.text: Normal browsing instantly produces DNS lookups, then TCP/TLS or UDP/QUIC connections, followed by application data
Click on a packet and expand the Hypertext Transfer Protocol section to see details of the request
section_anchors.expected_section: Part 3: Identify HTTP Requests and Responses

section_anchors.text: Hosts selected: 1. Harvard.edu 2. Mit.edu 3. Utexas.edu 4. www.ust.hk 5. Ethz.ch
U.S. destinations generally have lower RTTs than overseas ones; transoceanic links produce the largest hop-to-hop increases in traceroute
Time between SYN and SYN, ACK: 0.025380
section_anchors.expected_section: Part 7: TCP 3-Way Handshake and Packet Timing

section_anchors.text: The capture shows a contemporary web stack where encryption and UDP-based transports are first-class
Custom columns for ports and HTTP fields, plus simple coloring rules, significantly speed up triage

notes: Manually validated against markdown_evaluation/pdfs/Assignment1.pdf on 2026-04-02 using pdftotext -layout and visual inspection of rendered pages. Screenshots are unnumbered in the source PDF, so gold figures/tables are intentionally empty.
